channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest Brussels posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: Belgian historian chosen to herald during Brussels Ommegang
item.description: Bart Van Loo will narrate the history of Brussels, Emperor Charles V, and the Burgundian Netherlands for spectators during Ommegang 2026 celebrations.

item.title: Princess Elisabeth on life at Harvard and what she misses most about Belgium
item.description: The Belgian princess has given her first real interview to the written press, speaking to Le Soir and three other Belgian newspapers just before her graduation from Harvard University.

item.title: Mother arrested for attempting to kill her three children in Uccle
item.description: Neighbours called emergency services around 20:30 after hearing screams at the family's home on Avenue Brugmann in Brussels.

item.title: Brussels Government approves 'coercive control' of Saint-Josse municipality
item.description: In addition to the financial problems, serious shortcomings were also found at the Saint-Josse social housing agency.

item.title: Two key train lines around Brussels will not run this weekend
item.description: This is due to maintenance works by the railway infrastructure operator Infrabel.

item.title: Brussels police conduct searches at social housing operator in Anderlecht
item.description: The Foyer Anderlechtois has been searched in light of the Flemish public media's investigation into the allocation of social housing in Anderlecht.

item.title: Brussels one step closer to new public open-air rooftop swimming pool
item.description: The open-air swimming pool is expected to have a surface area similar to that of Brussels' Grand Place.

item.title: Brussels for kids: What to do with your family this weekend – 30 to 31 May
item.description: From parades and festivals to circus classes and urban farms visits, there are lots of fun activities for kids to enjoy in Brussels this weekend.

item.title: Housing costs squeeze household budgets in Brussels as rents and property prices soar
item.description: Brussels families spend a significantly higher share of their household budgets on housing than those in Flanders and Wallonia.

item.title: Brussels teenager arrested after fight on coastal tram
item.description: Several De Lijn staff and police officers were injured on Sunday evening during a fight on a tram.

item.title: Brussels artisanal bakery bought by French company
item.description: La Fleur du Pain was founded in 1990 by Laurent Ricard. The chain has five branches in the capital and one in Groot-Bijgaarden, on the outskirts of Brussels.

item.title: Royal Palace reopens this summer after two-year renovation
item.description: From 3 July to 16 August, visitors can explore areas such as the Throne Room, the Great Gallery, and the gardens.

item.title: Saint-Josse could be placed under control of Brussels Government due to spiralling debts
item.description: "We cannot accept that a municipality simply does whatever it wants," said State Secretary Karine Lalieux (PS).

item.title: Brussels' STIB is no longer recruiting staff
item.description: Despite the lack of recruits, the company must maintain current service levels for buses, trams, and metros in the capital.

item.title: More Belgian municipalities increase property taxes
item.description: In 2026, 88 Belgian municipalities increased the surcharges, a notable jump from just six municipalities last year.

item.title: House door damaged in overnight explosion in Etterbeek
item.description: An explosion occurred overnight from Tuesday to Wednesday in front of a house in Etterbeek, Brussels firefighters reported on Wednesday morning.

item.title: Two more major Brussels tram lines disrupted this summer
item.description: "We are aware that these interruptions are inconvenient, but they are necessary".

item.title: 'I saw you, get lost': Women confront man masturbating in Brussels park
item.description: Several women confronted a man in Forest Park on Friday, who they claim was pleasuring himself in front of them.

item.title: Power restored after outage across several Brussels municipalities
item.description: The network operator for the distribution of natural gas and electricity in Brussels, Sibelga, reported outages in three municipalities.

item.title: Up to 32°C in Brussels: Is Belgium heading toward first heatwave of 2026?
item.description: Belgium has been plagued by rain, hail and thunderstorms in recent months, but the country has been experiencing exactly the opposite the past couple of days.
